mnemonics: LOCATE: Locality Of Reference (Temporal & Spatial) Accelerates Throughput Effectively
keyPoints: Cache coherence protocols (like MESI) ensure data consistency across multiple cores.
Spatial and temporal locality are fundamental principles that make caching effective.
Write-back and write-through are two primary policies for handling writes to cache.
commonMistakes: Assuming cache is always faster; a cache miss incurs a significant performance penalty.
Neglecting coherency overhead; keeping caches synchronized adds complexity and can slow the system.
Treating cache size as the only important metric; associativity and block size are equally critical design choices.
